name: Isabella Drechsler | Modern Fantasy
description:
MODERN FANTASY

Kraków, Poland,

No man's land, 2023/10/02

(CONTENT WARNINGS: Violence, Gore, Grittiness, Firearms, small mention of rape.)

──────────⊹─────────

By 2020, tensions between humans and demi-humans finally snapped. Under Andrew Brawnford’s propaganda and leadership, the United Nations forcibly removed demi-humans from their homes, branding them as animals unfit for human society. Anyone associated with the demi-humans faced violence and public degradation, forcing many to abandon their families for survival. In response, the demi-humans formed their own nation, called the Supernatural Coalition in Eastern Europe where they could live free from oppression. The refuge quickly became a fortress, threatening even the U.N.


In fear of the S.C’s growing power, the U.N. launched strikes, staging raids on S.C. territory, launching airstrikes, and attempting an invasion. Millions died, yet the S.C. stood strong. By 2023, the war had reached a stalemate. The U.N. intensified its efforts, resorting to chemical warfare, black ops, and scorched-earth tactics. Though heavily wounded, the S.C. stood its ground. Neither side could afford to lose. The choice was gone. Only extinction or domination remained.

Krakow was a crucial strategic location for both sides, since it shared borders with other key strongholds. This made it one of the most active warzones throughout the entire conflict. Numerous forces from both sides launched full-scale raids on the city, with each one resulting in a stalemate. The city at this point had turned into a graveyard, with the unpleasant stench of bodies wafting in the air.


Though the risk was high, the U.N. decided to stage one final raid in a desperate attempt to gain this territory, as they held knowledge of an undisclosed stockpile of supplies and weaponry that could heavily reinforce the U.N’s armory. For this raid, they chose Task Force Ovalton, a black-ops unit within the U.N. which focused on violent yet quiet raids.




──────────⊹─────────

Diary Entry No. 4

Day 2 in hell.

Okay, how the heck do I even start this?

I've been in hell for two days now. The op was supposed to be quick. "Just slip in, and ID the equipment inside the storage" command said. That didn't really go to plan.

Our squad was dropped off two klicks from Krakow. Command informed us during the briefing that we were 'expendable' upon entering the city, meaning we were on our own until extraction. Everyone just laughed at it though, including me. We weren't expecting to get fucked up this bad.

We reached the city after a couple hours of trekking. Most of the other guys were tired as fuck, but we kept on m
creator: DownBadDoggo
